title: Hash Maps
subtitle: O(1) average-case lookup, insert, and delete — the most powerful interview tool

nodes.label: Hash Maps
nodes.description: Key-value store using hash function to compute array index — O(1) on average for all operations

nodes.label: How It Works
nodes.description: Understanding the mechanics to answer 'how would you implement'

nodes.label: Hash Function
items: String hash: sum of char * prime positions

nodes.label: Collision Handling
items: Chaining: each bucket is a linked list
Open Addressing: probe next empty slot
Java HashMap: chaining + tree at 8 items

nodes.label: Complexity
items: Worst case (all hash to same bucket): O(n)

nodes.label: Problem Patterns
nodes.description: HashMap patterns that unlock dozens of problems

nodes.label: Frequency Count
items: Problems: Valid Anagram, Word Frequency, Group Anagrams

nodes.label: Complement Lookup
items: Store seen elements as you iterate
Check if complement = target - current exists
Single pass: O(n) time, O(n) space
Problems: Two Sum, Four Sum, Pair with Target

nodes.label: Prefix Sum + Map
items: Subarray sum = k: check map[currentSum - k]

nodes.label: Memoization / DP Cache
items: Problems: Word Break, Coin Change, LRU Cache

nodes.label: Variants
nodes.description: Specialized map structures for specific needs

nodes.label: TreeMap / SortedMap
items: O(log n) get/put vs O(1) HashMap
Use when: need ordering or range queries
